aliases: redis standalone vs sentinel vs cluster docker

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Redis 8.0 (2025) unified modules into core; Redis 7.0 introduced multi-part AOF. Docker Compose v2 replaced v1.

constraints: Redis Cluster requires a MINIMUM of 3 master nodes -- fewer masters cannot form a quorum for failover
Both the data port (default 6379) AND cluster bus port (data port + 10000) MUST be accessible between all nodes
Redis Cluster does NOT support NATted environments by default -- use host networking or cluster-announce-ip/port directives
NEVER use the default Docker bridge network for Redis Cluster -- use a user-defined bridge or host networking
cluster-config-file (nodes.conf) MUST persist across container restarts via Docker volumes
Multi-key operations (MGET, MSET, transactions) only work on keys in the same hash slot -- use hash tags {tag} to co-locate

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need a simple Redis cache without HA or sharding
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use standalone Redis: docker run redis:7.4-alpine

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need Redis with search, JSON, or time series modules in cluster mode
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use redis/redis-stack-server image with cluster config

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Using managed Redis (AWS ElastiCache, GCP Memorystore, Azure Cache)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Refer to cloud provider Redis cluster documentation

inputs_needed.question: Which Redis deployment mode do you need?
What level of data persistence do you need?
